vulnerability.reason: generated describes technical detection limitations, while reference describes platform liability shields incentivizing minimal investment, a related but different sensitivity condition.
vulnerability.reference: Platform liability shields incentivize minimal detection investment, externalizing harm to buyers.
vulnerability.generated: Platform content screening relies on automated detectors with limited accuracy against varied AI-generated image characteristics.

impact.reason: legal harm is mentioned, but the affected party (brands) and the specific false advertising claims are missing.
impact.reference: Commercial use of synthetic images in advertising triggers false advertising claims and legal liability for brands.
impact.generated: Commercial image repositories become contaminated with synthetic images, eroding buyer trust and causing financial and legal harm.

dose_response.reason: the initial causal steps (improved quality to indistinguishable) are present, but the arc omits commercial use and false advertising, ending instead with repository contamination and general trust/harm.
dose_response.reference: Improved text-to-image generation quality enables images indistinguishable by automated detectors, which in digital asset platforms screening AI-generated stock photos exposes operators and buyers, leading to commercial use of synthetic images triggering false advertising claims.
dose_response.generated: AI-generated images indistinguishable from real images by automated detectors due to improved generation quality, through content integrity screening reliance, triggers limited accuracy against varied AI-generated image characteristics, leading to contaminated repositories, eroding trust and causing financial and legal harm.
